svg.title: Full TTS Evaluation Metrics Taxonomy from TTSDS and TTSDS2
svg.desc: Complete taxonomy of subjective and objective TTS evaluation metrics including all metrics from the TTSDS and TTSDS2 papers

g.text: Human listeners rate audio directly — gold standard but slow, expensive, non-comparable across studies
Most widely used subjective metric in TTS.
Comparative MOS, −3 to +3 scale vs reference.
Pairwise; fewer raters needed than MOS.
Multi-stimulus hidden reference + anchors. Listeners rate several systems simultaneously (0–100). Reveals fine-grained rank differences.
Computed automatically — no human listeners required
Compare synthesised audio to a matching ground-truth waveform — unavailable at inference time
B · MOS prediction networks (non-intrusive unless noted)
Neural networks trained to predict MOS without human raters
and PESQ jointly from a single model.
Clusters rather than a continuous scale.
C · Speaker similarity metrics (non-intrusive, non-matching ref)
Embed speech into a speaker verification space; cosine similarity to a reference speaker
Compare feature distributions of many samples — no matching reference required
Meta model scoring 4 axes: AE-CE = Content Enjoyment, AE-CU = Content Usefulness,
AE-PQ = Production Quality, AE-Complexity. Reference-free; best on clean/noisy domains.
ASR transcript vs ground-truth. Measures intelligibility; no reference audio needed.
Character-level WER; finer-grained, better for tonal/morphologically rich languages.
★ = proposed metric · All TTSDS2 spearman correlations from Table 3 (Minixhofer et al. 2026, arXiv:2506.19441)
